title: Death Certificate: Afghan U.S. Military Ally Paktiawal Died of Allergic Reaction in ICE Custody
excerpt: A death certificate obtained by the Associated Press revealed that Mohammad Nazeer Paktiawal, an Afghan national who fought alongside U.S. Special Forces, died of anaphylaxis in ICE custody just one day after being detained — the first ICE custody death in the Trump second term to be ruled an accident. His wife's attempt to provide his asthma inhaler when he was arrested was reportedly rejected by ICE agents.
content:
On July 6, 2026, the Associated Press published details from the death certificate of Mohammad Nazeer Paktiawal, 41, an Afghan national who had fought alongside U.S. Special Forces for a decade before being resettled in the United States. Paktiawal died on March 14, 2026 at Parkland Memorial Hospital in Dallas, just one day after ICE arrested him at his Richardson, Texas home while he was getting his children ready for school.

The death certificate lists the cause of death as "anaphylaxis complicating acute asthma exacerbation," triggered by an adverse drug reaction to an unidentified substance. ICE's own intake report stated that Paktiawal denied any medical conditions or allergies — yet his wife had attempted to hand agents his asthma inhaler when he was taken into custody, and agents rejected the device. The Dallas County Medical Examiner's Office refused to release the full autopsy, citing a pending federal criminal investigation.

  • Paktiawal entered the U.S. legally through a humanitarian evacuation process in 2021 and had a pending asylum application at the time of his arrest.
  • He had not been convicted of any crime, though ICE cited prior arrests for food stamp fraud and theft to justify his targeting for deportation.
  • His death is the first of more than 50 ICE custody deaths during the second Trump administration to be ruled an accident, per AP tracking.
  • Advocacy group AfghanEvac and members of Congress demanded the release of the autopsy, with Sen. Richard Blumenthal stating: "What's happening here smacks of a cover-up."
  • A second autopsy commissioned by the family could not confirm key toxicology findings because no blood remained for testing.
mediaPlaceholder: Death certificate showing cause of death as anaphylaxis; family photo of Mohammad Nazeer Paktiawal provided by AfghanEvac
imageAlt: Death Certificate: Afghan U.S. Military Ally Paktiawal Died of Allergic Reaction in ICE Custody
